codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix handoff/transcript skeleton plus both relevant diffs. The original blocking false-positive path from `pairing-security-fix-exact-serve-route-verification` is resolved: `proxy_targets_bridge_loopback()` now accepts only loopback HTTP backends on port 3110, so HTTPS loopback targets no longer satisfy `pairing_base_url_from_tailscale_status()`, while the existing exact MagicDNS HTTPS front-door verification remains intact.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failed review, the fix feature's transcript skeleton and handoff, and both commit diffs. The new change directly removes acceptance of `https://127.0.0.1:3110`, `https://localhost:3110`, and `https://[::1]:3110` as valid Serve proxy targets, and the added regression test covers those cases while preserving acceptance of the verified MagicDNS HTTPS route that proxies to loopback HTTP on 3110. I did not find new blocking issues in scope.
